xanesnet.datasets package¶

Dataset package public API.

class xanesnet.datasets.Dataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset, ABC

Abstract base class for prepared on-disk datasets.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw data source that yields structures, spectra, and metadata.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to load processed samples into memory after preparation.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional train/validation/test split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to serialized split indices.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile)[source]¶

Initialize shared dataset state.

prepare()[source]¶

Process raw datasource items sequentially.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True when preparation or reuse of existing files succeeded.

check_preload()[source]¶

Load processed samples into memory when preloading is enabled.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

Whether the dataset is configured for in-memory preloading.

setup_splits()[source]¶

Create subsets from an index file or configured split ratios.

Return type:

None

get_subset_indices(index)[source]¶

Return indices for one configured subset.

Parameters:

index (int) – Subset position in self.subsets.

Return type:

list[int] | None

Returns:

Subset indices, or None when index is out of range.

get_all_subset_indices()[source]¶

Return indices for all configured subsets.

Return type:

list[list[int]]

Returns:

A list of subset index lists.

get_subset(index)[source]¶

Return one configured subset.

Parameters:

index (int) – Subset position in self.subsets.

Return type:

Subset | None

Returns:

The requested subset, or None when index is out of range.

property subsets: list[Subset]¶

Configured dataset subsets.

property train_subset: Subset | None¶

Training subset, when configured.

property valid_subset: Subset | None¶

Validation subset, when configured.

get_dataloader()[source]¶

Return the dataloader class for this dataset.

Return type:

type[DataLoader]

Returns:

The dataloader class expected by training and inference code.

abstractmethod collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate raw items into one dataloader batch.

Parameters:

batch (list[Any]) – Samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

A framework-specific batch object.

__len__()[source]¶

Return the number of processed samples.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Number of processed samples available on disk or in memory.

__getitem__(idx)[source]¶

Return one processed sample.

Parameters:

idx (int) – Processed sample index.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

Loaded sample from memory or disk.

abstract property signature: Config¶

Dataset configuration signature.

Returns:

Configuration values that identify this dataset instance.

property processed_dir: str¶

Path to the processed data directory.

property processed_files: list[str]¶

Processed file paths in canonical sample order.

class xanesnet.datasets.TorchDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset

Dataset base class for standard PyTorch dataloaders.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw data source used during preparation.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile)[source]¶

Initialize a standard PyTorch dataset.

collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate samples with PyTorch’s default collator.

Parameters:

batch (list[Any]) – Samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

Batch produced by default_collate.

class xanesnet.datasets.TorchGeometricDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset, Dataset

Dataset base class for PyTorch Geometric dataloaders.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw data source used during preparation.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile)[source]¶

Initialize a PyTorch Geometric dataset.

collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate graph samples into a PyG batch.

Parameters:

batch (list[BaseData]) – Graph samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Batch

Returns:

PyG batch created by Batch.from_data_list.

class xanesnet.datasets.DescriptorDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, mode, fourier, fourier_concat, gaussian, widths_eV, basis_stride, basis_path, descriptors)[source]¶

Bases: TorchDataset

Dataset that converts structures to descriptor tensors.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource of pymatgen structures or molecules.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • mode (str) – forward for descriptor-to-spectrum or reverse for spectrum-to-descriptor.

  • fourier (bool) – Whether to add Fourier-transformed spectra.

  • fourier_concat (bool) – Whether Fourier features concatenate real and imaginary components.

  • gaussian (bool) – Whether to fit spectra to a Gaussian basis.

  • widths_eV (list[float]) – Gaussian basis widths in eV.

  • basis_stride (int) – Energy-grid stride used when creating a Gaussian basis.

  • basis_path (str | None) – Optional serialized spectral basis path.

  • descriptors (list[Config]) – Descriptor configuration objects.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, mode, fourier, fourier_concat, gaussian, widths_eV, basis_stride, basis_path, descriptors)[source]¶

Initialize the descriptor dataset.

collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate descriptor samples into a batch.

Parameters:

batch (list[DescriptorData]) – Descriptor samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

DescriptorData

Returns:

Batched descriptor data with stacked tensor fields.

property signature: Config¶

Dataset configuration signature.

Returns:

Configuration values that identify this descriptor dataset.

class xanesnet.datasets.DatasetRegistry[source]¶

Bases: object

Name-based registry for dataset classes.

classmethod register(name)[source]¶

Register a dataset class under name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

Callable[[TypeVar(_DatasetT, bound= type[Dataset])], TypeVar(_DatasetT, bound= type[Dataset])]

Returns:

Decorator that registers and returns the class unchanged.

Raises:

KeyError – If name is already registered.

classmethod get(name)[source]¶

Return the dataset class registered as name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

type[Dataset]

Returns:

Registered dataset class.

Raises:

KeyError – If no dataset is registered under name.

classmethod list()[source]¶

Return all registered dataset names.

Return type:

list[str]

Returns:

Registry keys in insertion order.

class xanesnet.datasets.DescriptorData(x=None, y=None, energies=None, fourier=None, c_star=None, file_name=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Container for one descriptor dataset sample or batch.

x¶

Model input tensor, commonly (n_features,) or (batch, n_features).

y¶

Model target tensor, commonly (n_energies,) or (batch, n_energies).

energies¶

Energy grid tensor with shape (n_energies,) or (batch, n_energies).

fourier¶

Optional Fourier feature tensor.

c_star¶

Optional Gaussian basis coefficient tensor.

file_name¶

Source file name metadata for one sample or a batch.

y: Tensor | None = None¶
energies: Tensor | None = None¶

class xanesnet.datasets.GemNetDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, quadruplets, int_cutoff, int_max_neighbors=None, int_graph_method=None, int_min_facet_area=None, int_cov_radii_scale=None, oc_mode=False, oc_cutoff_aeaint=None, oc_cutoff_aint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aeaint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aint=None, oc_graph_method_aeaint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aeaint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aeaint=None, oc_graph_method_aint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aint=None)[source]¶

Bases: TorchGeometricDataset

Dataset for GemNet and GemNet-OC graph inputs.

Mixing graph methods is supported (e.g. graph_method="voronoi" for a compact main graph paired with int_graph_method="radius" for a larger interaction graph). A derived graph is reused from a previously-built one only when all of (cutoff, max_num_neighbors, method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale) match; otherwise it is built from scratch.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource of pymatgen structures or molecules.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Main graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • graph_method (str) – Main graph construction method.

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional Voronoi facet-area threshold for the main graph.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Covalent-radii scale for the main graph.

  • quadruplets (bool) – Whether to compute quadruplet indices.

  • int_cutoff (float | None) – Interaction graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • int_max_neighbors (int | None) – Optional interaction graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • int_graph_method (str | None) – Optional interaction graph construction method.

  • int_min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional interaction Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • int_cov_radii_scale (float | None) – Optional interaction covalent-radii scale.

  • oc_mode (bool) – Whether to precompute GemNet-OC auxiliary graphs and mixed triplets.

  • oc_cutoff_aeaint (float | None) – Atom-edge-atom graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • oc_cutoff_aint (float | None) – Atom-atom graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • oc_max_neighbors_aeaint (int | None) – Atom-edge-atom graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • oc_max_neighbors_aint (int | None) – Atom-atom graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • oc_graph_method_aeaint (str | None) – Atom-edge-atom graph construction method override.

  • oc_min_facet_area_aeaint (float | str | None) – Atom-edge-atom Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • oc_cov_radii_scale_aeaint (float | None) – Atom-edge-atom covalent-radii scale.

  • oc_graph_method_aint (str | None) – Atom-atom graph construction method override.

  • oc_min_facet_area_aint (float | str | None) – Atom-atom Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • oc_cov_radii_scale_aint (float | None) – Atom-atom covalent-radii scale.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, quadruplets, int_cutoff, int_max_neighbors=None, int_graph_method=None, int_min_facet_area=None, int_cov_radii_scale=None, oc_mode=False, oc_cutoff_aeaint=None, oc_cutoff_aint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aeaint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aint=None, oc_graph_method_aeaint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aeaint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aeaint=None, oc_graph_method_aint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aint=None)[source]¶

Initialize the GemNet dataset.

Collate GemNet graph samples into one PyG batch.

Parameters:

batch (list[BaseData]) – GemNet graph samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Batch

Returns:

PyG batch with target tensors concatenated over absorber sites.

class xanesnet.datasets.GemNetData(x=None, edge_index=None, edge_attr=None, y=None, pos=None, time=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Data

PyG Data subclass for GemNet and GemNet-OC samples.

The custom __inc__ is critical for correct PyG batching of all edge / triplet / quadruplet / intermediate indices across multiple graphs.

__inc__(key, value, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Return PyG batching increments for GemNet index fields.

Parameters:

  • key (str) – Data attribute currently being batched.

  • value (Any) – Attribute value currently being batched.

  • *args (Any) – Additional PyG arguments.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional PyG keyword arguments.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

Increment used by PyG for key.

__cat_dim__(key, value, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Return the concatenation dimension for GemNet graph fields.

Parameters:

  • key (str) – Data attribute currently being batched.

  • value (Any) – Attribute value currently being batched.

  • *args (Any) – Additional PyG arguments.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional PyG keyword arguments.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

Concatenation dimension used by PyG for key.

class xanesnet.datasets.E3EEDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, use_path_branch, max_paths_per_structure, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, att_cutoff, att_max_num_neighbors, att_graph_method, att_min_facet_area, att_cov_radii_scale)[source]¶

Bases: TorchGeometricDataset

E3EE dataset that emits one graph sample per absorber site.

The dataset preserves absorber ordering and supports both periodic Structures and non-periodic Molecules. All edges and absorber- centred triplet path scalars are precomputed at prepare() time using the shared graph utilities, so the model does not need to rebuild geometry at forward time.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource of pymatgen structures or molecules.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Main graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • use_path_branch (bool) – Whether to precompute absorber-centered paths.

  • max_paths_per_structure (int) – Maximum absorber paths saved per structure.

  • graph_method (str) – Main graph construction method.

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Covalent-radii scale for graph construction.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • att_max_num_neighbors (int) – Attention graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • att_graph_method (str) – Attention graph construction method.

  • att_min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional attention Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • att_cov_radii_scale (float) – Attention graph covalent-radii scale.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, use_path_branch, max_paths_per_structure, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, att_cutoff, att_max_num_neighbors, att_graph_method, att_min_facet_area, att_cov_radii_scale)[source]¶

Initialize the E3EE dataset.

collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate absorber-centered graph samples into one padded batch.

Node tensors are padded to (batch, max_nodes, ...) and flat edge/path indices are offset by batch_index * max_nodes.

Parameters:

batch (list[BaseData]) – PyG graph samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Batch

Returns:

PyG batch with E3EE-specific padded tensors attached.

property signature: Config¶

Dataset configuration signature.

Returns:

Configuration values that identify this E3EE dataset.

class xanesnet.datasets.EnvEmbedData(descriptor_features=None, distance_features=None, intensities=None, energies=None, c_star=None, lengths=None, file_name=None, basis=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Container for one environment-embedding sample or batch.

descriptor_features¶

Per-site descriptor tensor with shape (n_sites, n_features) or (batch, max_sites, n_features).

distance_features¶

Per-site absorber distance tensor with shape (n_sites,) or (batch, max_sites) in Angstrom.

intensities¶

Spectrum intensity tensor with shape (n_energies,) or (batch, n_energies).

energies¶

Energy grid tensor with shape (n_energies,) or (batch, n_energies).

c_star¶

Gaussian basis coefficient tensor.

lengths¶

Original per-sample site counts for padded batches.

file_name¶

Source file name metadata for one sample or a batch.

basis¶

Spectral basis attached at runtime and excluded from saved state.

descriptor_features: Tensor | None = None¶
distance_features: Tensor | None = None¶
intensities: Tensor | None = None¶
energies: Tensor | None = None¶
c_star: Tensor | None = None¶
lengths: Tensor | None = None¶
file_name: str | list[Any] | None = None¶
basis: SpectralBasis | None = None¶

class xanesnet.datasets.EnvEmbedDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, widths_eV, basis_stride, basis_path, env_radius, descriptors)[source]¶

Bases: TorchDataset

Dataset that creates absorber-centered environment embeddings.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource of pymatgen structures or molecules.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • widths_eV (list[float]) – Gaussian basis widths in eV.

  • basis_stride (int) – Energy-grid stride used when creating a Gaussian basis.

  • basis_path (str | None) – Optional serialized spectral basis path.

  • env_radius (float | None) – Periodic-neighbor cutoff in Angstrom for structures.

  • descriptors (list[Config]) – Descriptor configuration objects.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, widths_eV, basis_stride, basis_path, env_radius, descriptors)[source]¶

Initialize the environment-embedding dataset.

basis: SpectralBasis | None¶

EnvEmbedData

class xanesnet.datasets.E3EEBatch(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Protocol

Protocol for batches emitted by E3EEDataset.collate_fn.

Node fields are padded to (batch, max_nodes, ...). Flat edge and path indices are offset into the padded batch * max_nodes layout.

class xanesnet.datasets.E3EEFullBatch(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Protocol

Protocol for batches emitted by E3EEFullDataset.collate_fn.

Node fields are padded to (batch, max_nodes, ...). Targets are concatenated over absorber sites across the batch.

x: Tensor¶
mask: Tensor¶
absorber_mask: Tensor¶
edge_src: Tensor¶
edge_dst: Tensor¶
edge_weight: Tensor¶
edge_vec: Tensor¶
att_src: Tensor¶
att_dst: Tensor¶
att_dist: Tensor¶
att_vec: Tensor¶
path_center: Tensor¶
path_j: Tensor¶
path_k: Tensor¶
path_r0j: Tensor¶
path_r0k: Tensor¶
path_rjk: Tensor¶
path_cosangle: Tensor¶
energies: Tensor¶
intensities: Tensor¶

__init__(*args, **kwargs)¶
class xanesnet.datasets.E3EEFullDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, use_path_branch, max_paths_per_site, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, att_cutoff, att_max_num_neighbors, att_graph_method, att_min_facet_area, att_cov_radii_scale, use_absorber_mask)[source]¶

Bases: TorchGeometricDataset

Full-structure E3EE dataset that emits one graph per structure.

The dataset supports periodic and non-periodic structures and predicts a spectrum for every atom. Atoms with a ground-truth spectrum are flagged in absorber_mask; the training loop selects those rows via the mask (same pattern as SchNet / DimeNet).

All edges are computed once per structure. Absorber-centred triplet paths are computed for every site independently (with max_paths_per_site paths each) and tagged with path_center so that the model can scatter them into the per-atom layout.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource of pymatgen structures or molecules.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Main graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • use_path_branch (bool) – Whether to precompute site-centered paths.

  • max_paths_per_site (int) – Maximum paths saved per site.

  • graph_method (str) – Main graph construction method.

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Covalent-radii scale for graph construction.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • att_max_num_neighbors (int) – Attention graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • att_graph_method (str) – Attention graph construction method.

  • att_min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional attention Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • att_cov_radii_scale (float) – Attention graph covalent-radii scale.

  • use_absorber_mask (bool) – Whether attention/path data are limited to absorber sites.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, use_path_branch, max_paths_per_site, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, att_cutoff, att_max_num_neighbors, att_graph_method, att_min_facet_area, att_cov_radii_scale, use_absorber_mask)[source]¶

Initialize the full-structure E3EE dataset.

collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate full-structure graph samples into one padded batch.

Node tensors are padded to (batch, max_nodes, ...) and flat edge/path indices, including path_center, are offset by batch_index * max_nodes.

Parameters:

batch (list[BaseData]) – PyG graph samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Batch

Returns:

PyG batch with full-structure E3EE tensors attached.

property signature: Config¶

Dataset configuration signature.

Returns:

Configuration values that identify this full-structure E3EE dataset.

Bases: Data

PyG data object with custom batching increments for triplet indices.

__inc__(key, value, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Return PyG batching increments for graph index fields.

Parameters:

  • key (str) – Data attribute currently being batched.

  • value (Any) – Attribute value currently being batched.

  • *args (Any) – Additional PyG arguments.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional PyG keyword arguments.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

Increment used by PyG for key.

class xanesnet.datasets.GeometryGraphDataset(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, compute_angles, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale)[source]¶

Bases: TorchGeometricDataset

Geometry-based graph dataset.

Supports two edge construction methods:

  • graph_method="radius": distance-cutoff radius graph.

  • graph_method="voronoi": Voronoi-tessellation graph (still bounded by cutoff; Voronoi neighbors with distances above cutoff are dropped).

In both cases edge_weight is the Cartesian edge length, and the returned graph is bidirectional (see xanesnet.utils.graph).

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource of pymatgen structures or molecules.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • cutoff (float) – Graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Per-source neighbor cap.

  • compute_angles (bool) – Whether to precompute triplet angles.

  • graph_method (str) – Graph construction method.

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Covalent-radii scale for graph construction.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, compute_angles, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale)[source]¶

Initialize the geometry graph dataset.

collate_fn(batch)[source]¶

Collate geometry graph samples into one PyG batch.

Parameters:

batch (list[BaseData]) – Geometry graph samples loaded by __getitem__.

Return type:

Batch

Returns:

PyG batch with target tensors concatenated over absorber sites.

property signature: Config¶

Dataset configuration signature.

Returns:

Configuration values that identify this geometry graph dataset.

class xanesnet.datasets.DescriptorDatasetMp(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, mode, fourier, fourier_concat, gaussian, widths_eV, basis_stride, basis_path, descriptors, num_workers)[source]¶

Bases: MpDatasetMixin, DescriptorDataset

Multiprocessing variant of DescriptorDataset.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource used during preparation.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • mode (str) – forward for descriptor-to-spectrum or reverse for spectrum-to-descriptor.

  • fourier (bool) – Whether to add Fourier-transformed spectra.

  • fourier_concat (bool) – Whether Fourier features concatenate real and imaginary components.

  • gaussian (bool) – Whether to fit spectra to a Gaussian basis.

  • widths_eV (list[float]) – Gaussian basis widths in eV.

  • basis_stride (int) – Energy-grid stride used when creating a Gaussian basis.

  • basis_path (str | None) – Optional serialized spectral basis path.

  • descriptors (list[Config]) – Descriptor configuration objects.

  • num_workers (int | None) – Requested worker process count.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, mode, fourier, fourier_concat, gaussian, widths_eV, basis_stride, basis_path, descriptors, num_workers)[source]¶

Initialize a multiprocessing descriptor dataset.

Bases: MpDatasetMixin, E3EEDataset

Multiprocessing variant of E3EEDataset. See the parent for semantics.

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource used during preparation.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Main graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • use_path_branch (bool) – Whether to precompute absorber-centered paths.

  • max_paths_per_structure (int) – Maximum absorber paths saved per structure.

  • graph_method (str) – Main graph construction method.

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Covalent-radii scale for graph construction.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • att_max_num_neighbors (int) – Attention graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • att_graph_method (str) – Attention graph construction method.

  • att_min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional attention Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • att_cov_radii_scale (float) – Attention graph covalent-radii scale.

  • num_workers (int | None) – Requested worker process count.

class xanesnet.datasets.GemNetDatasetMp(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, quadruplets, int_cutoff, int_max_neighbors=None, int_graph_method=None, int_min_facet_area=None, int_cov_radii_scale=None, oc_mode=False, oc_cutoff_aeaint=None, oc_cutoff_aint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aeaint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aint=None, oc_graph_method_aeaint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aeaint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aeaint=None, oc_graph_method_aint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aint=None, num_workers=None)[source]¶

Bases: MpDatasetMixin, GemNetDataset

Multiprocessing variant of GemNetDataset (covers GemNet and GemNet-OC).

Parameters:

  • dataset_type (str) – Registered dataset type name.

  • datasource (DataSource) – Raw datasource used during preparation.

  • root (str) – Directory that stores processed .pth files.

  • preload (bool) – Whether to preload processed samples.

  • skip_prepare (bool) – Whether to reuse existing processed files.

  • split_ratios (list[float] | None) – Optional split ratios.

  • split_indexfile (str | None) – Optional path to split indices.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Main graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • graph_method (str) – Main graph construction method.

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional Voronoi facet-area threshold for the main graph.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Covalent-radii scale for the main graph.

  • quadruplets (bool) – Whether to compute quadruplet indices.

  • int_cutoff (float | None) – Interaction graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • int_max_neighbors (int | None) – Optional interaction graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • int_graph_method (str | None) – Optional interaction graph construction method.

  • int_min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional interaction Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • int_cov_radii_scale (float | None) – Optional interaction covalent-radii scale.

  • oc_mode (bool) – Whether to precompute GemNet-OC auxiliary graphs and mixed triplets.

  • oc_cutoff_aeaint (float | None) – Atom-edge-atom graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • oc_cutoff_aint (float | None) – Atom-atom graph cutoff in Angstrom.

  • oc_max_neighbors_aeaint (int | None) – Atom-edge-atom graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • oc_max_neighbors_aint (int | None) – Atom-atom graph per-source neighbor cap.

  • oc_graph_method_aeaint (str | None) – Atom-edge-atom graph construction method override.

  • oc_min_facet_area_aeaint (float | str | None) – Atom-edge-atom Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • oc_cov_radii_scale_aeaint (float | None) – Atom-edge-atom covalent-radii scale.

  • oc_graph_method_aint (str | None) – Atom-atom graph construction method override.

  • oc_min_facet_area_aint (float | str | None) – Atom-atom Voronoi facet-area threshold.

  • oc_cov_radii_scale_aint (float | None) – Atom-atom covalent-radii scale.

  • num_workers (int | None) – Requested worker process count.

__init__(dataset_type, datasource, root, preload, skip_prepare, split_ratios, split_indexfile, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, graph_method, min_facet_area, cov_radii_scale, quadruplets, int_cutoff, int_max_neighbors=None, int_graph_method=None, int_min_facet_area=None, int_cov_radii_scale=None, oc_mode=False, oc_cutoff_aeaint=None, oc_cutoff_aint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aeaint=None, oc_max_neighbors_aint=None, oc_graph_method_aeaint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aeaint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aeaint=None, oc_graph_method_aint=None, oc_min_facet_area_aint=None, oc_cov_radii_scale_aint=None, num_workers=None)[source]¶

Initialize a multiprocessing GemNet dataset.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.datasets.registry module¶

Registry for dataset implementations.

class xanesnet.datasets.registry.DatasetRegistry[source]¶

Bases: object

Name-based registry for dataset classes.

classmethod register(name)[source]¶

Register a dataset class under name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

Callable[[TypeVar(_DatasetT, bound= type[Dataset])], TypeVar(_DatasetT, bound= type[Dataset])]

Returns:

Decorator that registers and returns the class unchanged.

Raises:

KeyError – If name is already registered.

classmethod get(name)[source]¶

Return the dataset class registered as name.

Parameters:

name (str) – Registry key. Matching is case-insensitive.

Return type:

type[Dataset]

Returns:

Registered dataset class.

Raises:

KeyError – If no dataset is registered under name.

classmethod list()[source]¶

Return all registered dataset names.

Return type:

list[str]

Returns:

Registry keys in insertion order.
